params.name: Atenism
params.description: Atenism was a religious reform promoted by the ancient Egyptian pharaoh Akhenaten. At the center of this reform was the worship of the sun god Aten, resembling a monotheistic belief system. Akhenaten forcibly propagated the worship of Aten, suppressing traditional polytheistic beliefs. He even changed his own name to express loyalty to Aten and established a new capital dedicated to the sun god. However, after Akhenaten's death, Egypt quickly reverted to the worship of multiple gods, and Atenism …
